data.title: Formstack Tutorial: Automate Documents & Forms Step-by-Step
data.excerpt: Learn how to build no-code workflows with Formstack Forms, Docs, and Sign. Follow our step-by-step guide to save hours on paperwork.

$media.alt: Formstack Tutorial: Automate Documents & Forms Step-by-Step

children.text: Tired of juggling multiple apps for forms, document creation, and signatures? I get it. That chaotic stack of tools adds unnecessary steps and delays. That's why I was genuinely excited to dive into Formstack—a single, powerful platform that brings all of that together. In this hands-on tutorial, I'll walk you through exactly how to use Formstack to build seamless, automated workflows from start to finish. Let's turn that paperwork headache into a thing of the past.
*Disclosure: This post contains affiliate links. If you purchase through them, I may earn a commission at no extra cost to you.*
Why I Chose Formstack for Workflow Automation
Before we jump into the 'how,' let's talk about the 'why.' After testing dozens of productivity tools, Formstack stands out because it solves a very specific, painful problem:
disconnected processes
. Instead of copying data from a Google Form into a Word doc and then sending that PDF for signature in another app, Formstack lets you connect all those steps visually. It’s a true
platform for end-to-end automation
The platform is built on three core pillars that work beautifully together:
Formstack Forms:
Go beyond basic surveys. Create dynamic, logic-based forms that branch based on answers, accept payments, and populate data automatically.
Formstack Documents (formerly WebMerge):
This is the document automation powerhouse. It takes the data you collect and merges it into any template—contracts, invoices, proposals, HR letters—formatted perfectly every time.
Formstack Sign:
Securely send those generated documents for legally binding electronic signatures. Track status, set reminders, and store everything.

The real magic happens when you connect them. A job application form can automatically generate a tailored offer letter and send it for signature, all without you touching a file. If you want to explore the core philosophy, read
why we recommend choosing Formstack
Setting Up Your First Formstack Form
Let's start at the beginning: collecting information. The drag-and-drop Form Builder is incredibly intuitive. Here’s how to create a form that’s both functional and intelligent.
Start a New Form:
After logging in, click "New Form." You can choose a blank canvas or one of hundreds of pre-built templates for things like event registration, customer feedback, or support tickets.
Add Your Fields:
From the left panel, drag in the fields you need. Standard fields include text boxes, dropdowns, and file uploads. But don't stop there—explore the
Advanced Fields
like the
Calculated Field
(which can total prices) or the
Lookup Field
(which can pull data from other forms or sources).
Build Intelligence with Logic:

This is where your form becomes "smart." Under the "Logic" tab, you can set conditions. For example, if a respondent selects "No" for "Do you have a driver's license?", you can hide the subsequent field asking for the license number. This keeps forms clean and relevant for each user.
Style and Brand:
Use the theme editor to upload your logo, change fonts, and adjust colors to match your brand. A professional-looking form builds trust with your audience.
Configure Submission Settings:

Decide what happens when someone hits "Submit." You can show a custom thank you message, redirect them to a specific URL, or trigger an email notification. You can also connect the data to your favorite apps (like Salesforce, Slack, or Google Sheets) using native integrations or through a service like
Zapier
For more advanced techniques and ideas, check out our
collection of Formstack tips and best practices
Generating Documents Automatically from Form Data
This is the step that truly eliminates manual work. Now that you have a form collecting rich data, let's create a document template that uses it.
Create a Template in Formstack Documents:
Navigate to the Documents section and start a new document. You can upload an existing PDF, Word, or PowerPoint template, or build one from scratch in the editor.
Insert Merge Fields:

In your template document, you'll place merge tags where data from your form should go. For example, `{{First_Name}}` or `{{Invoice_Amount}}`. These tags correspond directly to the field names from your Formstack Form.
Set Up Delivery Options:
Where should the finished document go? You can choose to have it emailed to the submitter, saved to a cloud storage service (Google Drive, Dropbox, Box), or sent directly to a printer.
Connect Form to Document:

This is the key link. In your Formstack Form settings, under "Integrations," find "Documents." You'll select the document template you just created and map the form fields to the merge tags in your template. It’s a simple point-and-click process.
Pro Tip:

Use document actions to add conditional content. In your template, you can use simple rules like `{% if {{Condition}} == "Yes" %} Show this extra paragraph {% endif %}` to include or exclude text based on the form answers. This creates personalized, professional documents every single time.
Collecting Signatures with Formstack Sign
The final piece of the puzzle is getting that beautifully generated document signed. Formstack Sign makes this part effortless and secure.
Create a Signature Request:
After a document is generated (either automatically from your form or manually), you can send it for signature. In the Documents section, find the generated document and select "Send for Signature."
Add Signers:
Enter the email addresses of everyone who needs to sign. You can set the signing order (signer 1 signs before signer 2) if needed.
Place Signature Fields:

You'll be taken to a simple editor. Drag and drop signature boxes, initials, date fields, and text boxes onto the exact spots in the document where they need to be filled out. You can assign each field to a specific signer.
Send and Track:

Once sent, Formstack Sign emails the signers a link. You can track the entire process in your dashboard—see when the email was opened, the document viewed, and the signature completed. Automated reminders ensure nothing falls through the cracks.
The signed PDF is automatically stored in your Formstack account and can be synced back to your storage drive. The entire
workflow from collection to signature
is now automated. The power is incredible—see the full suite in action
here and get started with a free trial
New & Standout Features You'll Love
Formstack isn't a static tool; it's constantly evolving to make your work simpler. Here are some features that particularly impressed me:
Formstack Suite:

The unified platform is its biggest strength. The visual workflow builder lets you map out your entire process—Forms → Documents → Signatures—on one canvas. You can see how data flows, add conditional steps, and create complex automations without writing a single line of code. It’s the no-code solution that truly delivers.
Payments Integration:

Easily accept payments within your forms using Stripe, PayPal, or Authorize.Net. Perfect for order forms, event registrations with tickets, or donation campaigns. The financial data seamlessly flows into your documents to generate invoices or receipts.
Advanced Routing and Approvals:

For internal processes like expense reports or leave requests, you can build multi-step approval workflows. The form data is routed to the correct manager for review and signature, with automatic notifications and status updates for everyone involved.
AI-Powered Text Generation:
Newer additions are leveraging AI to help you draft document content based on the context of the data you've collected, speeding up the creation of contracts or personalized communications.
Stay on top of all the latest updates and releases by following our
new features roundup
Who is Formstack Ideal For?
Based on my experience, Formstack is a game-changer for specific roles and teams who handle repetitive, data-driven processes.
Operations & Administrative Teams:

The quintessential users. If you're the person who collects data from multiple sources and turns it into actionable documents or reports, Formstack will give you back hours every week. Think onboarding packets, vendor agreements, or internal policy acknowledgments.
HR Departments:

Automate the entire employee lifecycle—from application forms and offer letters to onboarding documents and performance review templates. Using Formstack Sign ensures everything is compliant and stored digitally.
Sales & Account Management:
Generate proposals, quotes, and statements of work automatically from a client intake form. Get them signed faster and reduce the time from "yes" to "paid."
Marketing & Event Planners:

Run event registrations that automatically generate attendee lists, payment invoices, and personalized confirmation emails. Collect feedback post-event with a survey and merge the results into a report template.
Any Business Aiming for Digital Transformation:
If your goal is to go paperless and streamline customer or client experiences, Formstack provides the foundational tools to make that happen efficiently.
Curious if it's the right fit for your specific use case? Dive deeper into
how different teams leverage Formstack

Frequently Asked Questions
Q: Is Formstack really a no-code platform? Can someone without technical skills use it?

A: Absolutely. That's one of its strongest points. The entire interface—building forms, designing document templates, and mapping workflows—is visual and drag-and-drop. If you can use PowerPoint or Google Docs, you can use Formstack to build powerful automations. No developers required for standard workflows.
Q: How secure are the electronic signatures in Formstack Sign?

A: Formstack Sign is built for business and legal compliance. It meets strict standards like ESIGN, UETA, and eIDAS. Each signature request includes a full audit trail with timestamps, IP addresses, and authentication steps. The documents are encrypted in transit and at rest, making them legally binding and secure.
Q: Can I integrate Formstack with my existing software, like CRM or cloud storage?

A: Yes, Formstack shines here. It has direct, native integrations with hundreds of popular business apps including Salesforce, HubSpot, Google Workspace, Dropbox, and Slack. For everything else, its robust API and compatibility with automation tools like Zapier allow you to connect to virtually any system.
Q: What's the difference between buying the individual products (Forms, Docs, Sign) and the Suite?

A: While you can purchase them separately, the Formstack Suite is designed to provide the most value by seamlessly connecting all three tools. The Suite includes the visual workflow builder that lets you automate processes across all three products from a single dashboard, which is where the major time savings and efficiency gains come from. For full automation, the Suite is the way to go.
Q: Is there a limit to how many documents I can generate or signatures I can send?

A: Formstack's pricing plans are structured to scale with your needs. They typically include a set number of document merges and signature requests per month in each tier. If you anticipate very high volumes, you can discuss custom enterprise plans with their sales team to ensure you have the capacity you need without any hassle.

children.text: What is Formstack, and what can it automate?

Formstack is a no-code platform for digital work. It automates the collection of data through online forms, the generation of documents from that data (like contracts or invoices), and the collection of legally binding electronic signatures on those documents—all connected in seamless workflows.
Is Formstack compliant with legal standards for electronic signatures?

Yes, Formstack Sign is designed to be legally compliant and secure. It adheres to major e-signature laws like the ESIGN Act in the US and eIDAS in the EU, providing a full audit trail for each signed document.
Can Formstack replace tools like DocuSign or Adobe Acrobat Sign?

Formstack Sign is a robust e-signature solution within the broader Formstack platform. If you need signatures as part of a larger automated workflow that includes form data collection and document generation, Formstack offers an integrated advantage that dedicated signature tools do not. For standalone e-signature needs, it is also a powerful option.
